creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to commit-hook from comment #7)
Why has it become "best practice" to NOT involve the MAINTAINERS
of changes, or "impending doom" regarding their ports? Why would
so much as a "heads up" not be warranted for these changes --
especially sweeping changes?
In this case, there is NOT a direct replacement. So why do YOU
get to make the final decision on what's best for OUR ports?
In this case, there is NOT a one-size-fits-all solution.

This is NOT the best way to deal with this situation. Please
reverse these commits.

--Chris
